page: Microsoft Azure E96pds_v6 is an Epdsv6-series virtual machine built on the arm64 architecture. Powered by a dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM processor, this server features 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 672.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 5669 GB of bundled local NVMe storage. The system operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports premium storage.

In benchmark testing, the E96pds_v6 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance and memory throughput. Multi-core evaluations, including stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, place the instance in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read operations achieving top-tier results. This high memory performance is reflected in database operations, where Passmark database tests and Redis SET operations both rank in the top 10%. Single-core performance, however, is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores landing in the middle 50% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides high resource density and memory capacity without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. The combination of 96 dedicated physical cores, massive memory bandwidth, and substantial local storage makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive and data-heavy workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput Redis caching layers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks such as software compilation and decompression.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM (7.0 GB per core) and 5669 GB of local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with both read and write operations ranking in the top 10%. While single-core performance remains average, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and high-throughput Redis caching. The qualitative cost efficiency of its ARM architecture makes it a strong choice for resource-dense deployments. This instance is highly recommended for memory-intensive databases, large-scale web serving, and parallel data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 672 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 96 dedicated cores, 672 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 virtual machine delivering ninety-six dedicated cores, massive memory bandwidth, and extensive local storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by Microsoft Neoverse-N2 at 3.4 GHz
672 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core
5669 GB of bundled local storage with premium storage capabilities
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and parallel data processing
